creative_brief: Hero image: a dad and young daughter at a kitchen counter unpacking a brown paper bag of groceries — visible farm-branded labels, warm natural light. Below the fold, split-screen farm portraits of real partner farms (Rachel Ward's property, Collins Wild Caught boats) alongside close-up product shots. Earthy palette, no stock photography feel.
headline: "I always assumed the supermarket checked all that for me."
subhead: So did a Northern Beaches dad — until he learned his family's beef spent 21 days in cold storage after travelling 2,400km from a feedlot. He found a different way to feed his kids.
hero_copy: He didn't set out to change his grocery shop. He just wanted to know what was in the mince his daughter ate three nights a week. The answer wasn't on the label. It was in a supply chain built to hide the answer. FarmThru exists because families like his asked one question the big chains couldn't answer honestly: where does this actually come from? Now he orders online, collects from the Brookvale hub, and knows every cut traces back to a named regenerative farm — days old, not weeks.

sections.heading: The question that changes everything
sections.body: Most of us trust the system. We see "grass-fed" on a label and move on. But here's what that label doesn't say: supermarket beef averages 2,400km of road and 14–21 days of cold storage before it hits the shelf. The animal may have started on grass, then finished in a feedlot on grain, hormones, and antibiotics. FarmThru is different. 100% sourced from regenerative farms. 100% grass-fed beef. Animals raised on pasture — no hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing. Straight from their farm to our hub to your kitchen. Days old, not weeks old. We'll show you where your food comes from. That's not a tagline. It's a promise we back with every order.

sections.heading: You'll know the farms by name
sections.body: Rachel Ward — actor turned regenerative farming advocate — raises cattle on her Kempsey, NSW property. She's a key partner and supplier to FarmThru. Bundarra Farm supplies our pork belly, pork and fennel salami, and Little French Ham. Collins Wild Caught brings wild-caught Alaskan salmon and seasonal Australian seafood from the South Coast of NSW. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA produces carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Farmer Brown supplies our eggs from NSW. These aren't anonymous producers. They're people rebuilding soil, raising animals right, and feeding families who care enough to ask.

sections.heading: How it works — simpler than you think
sections.body: Order online from our regenerative range — beef, chicken, pork, salmon, eggs, dairy. Collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No commitment. No subscription lock-in. Order when you want. Every product traces to a named farm. Every farm follows regenerative practices. You're not just buying groceries. You're voting for a food system that doesn't need to hide behind a label.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the change
sections.body: FarmThru is opening ownership to the people who believe food should be honest. Through our equity crowdfunding campaign, you can be part of it — not just as a customer, but as a partner in building a farm-direct grocery store that puts regenerative food within reach of everyday families. Place a fully refundable $5 deposit to join the waitlist and be first to know when the campaign goes live. This is early. There's risk — like any growing business, outcomes aren't guaranteed. But if you've ever wanted to own a piece of something that feeds people better, this is your chance.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is considering raising funds through equity crowdfunding. Any investment carries risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. This content is not financial advice. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and read any offer document in full before deciding to invest. The $5 deposit is fully refundable and does not constitute an investment or obligation.
